2603.16642.title: When AI Navigates the Fog of War
2603.16642.paper_summary: Can AI reason about a war before its trajectory becomes historically obvious? Analyzing this capability is difficult because retrospective geopolitical prediction is heavily confounded by training-data leakage. We address this challenge through a temporally grounded case study of the early stages of the 2026 Middle East conflict, which unfolded after the training cutoff of current frontier models. We construct 11 critical temporal nodes, 42 node-specific verifiable questions, and 5 general exploratory questions, requiring models to reason only from information that would have been publicly available at each moment. This design substantially mitigates training-data leakage concerns, creating a setting well-suited for studying how models analyze an unfolding crisis under the fog of war, and provides, to our knowledge, the first temporally grounded analysis of LLM reasoning in an ongoing geopolitical conflict. Our analysis reveals three main findings. First, current state-of-the-art large language models often display a striking degree of strategic realism, reasoning beyond surface rhetoric toward deeper structural incentives. Second, this capability is uneven across domains: models are more reliable in economically and logistically structured settings than in politically ambiguous multi-actor environments. Finally, model narratives evolve over time, shifting from early expectations of rapid containment toward more systemic accounts of regional entrenchment and attritional de-escalation. Since the conflict remains ongoing at the time of writing, this work can serve as an archival snapshot of model reasoning during an unfolding geopolitical crisis, enabling future studies without the hindsight bias of retrospective analysis.
2603.16642.paper_summary_zh: AI 能否在戰爭的軌跡變得歷史明顯之前進行推理？分析這一能力是困難的，因為回顧性的地緣政治預測受到訓練數據洩漏的嚴重干擾。我們通過對 2026 年中東衝突早期階段的時間性案例研究來應對這一挑戰，該衝突在當前前沿模型的訓練截止日期之後展開。我們構建了 11 個關鍵時間節點、42 個節點特定的可驗證問題和 5 個一般探索性問題，要求模型僅根據每個時刻公開可用的信息進行推理。這一設計大大減輕了訓練數據洩漏的擔憂，創造了一個非常適合研究模型在戰爭迷霧中分析不斷發展的危機的環境，並提供了我們所知的第一個在持續地緣政治衝突中基於時間的 LLM 推理分析。我們的分析揭示了三個主要發現。首先，當前最先進的大型語言模型經常顯示出驚人的戰略現實主義，推理超越表面的修辭，朝向更深層的結構性激勵。其次，這一能力在不同領域之間不均衡：模型在經濟和後勤結構化的環境中比在政治模糊的多參與者環境中更可靠。最後，模型敘事隨著時間的推移而演變，從對快速遏制的早期預期轉向對區域鞏固和消耗性降級的更系統性描述。由於衝突在撰寫時仍在進行中，這項工作可以作為模型在不斷發展的地緣政治危機中推理的檔案快照，從而使未來的研究能夠避免回顧性分析的後見之明偏見。
2603.16642.author: Ming Li et.al.
2603.16642.authors: Ming Li, Xirui Li, Tianyi Zhou
